message:
perf(session): cache entry IDs in HashSet for O(1) uniqueness checks

Replace the linear-scan fast path (from the previous commit) with a
persistent HashSet<String> field (entry_ids) on the Session struct that
is maintained incrementally as entries are appended. This eliminates both
the O(n) linear scan per ID check AND the O(n) HashSet rebuild that was
the fallback path.

Every append method (append_message, append_compaction, append_custom_entry,
append_session_info, append_model_change, append_thinking_level_change,
append_branch_summary, add_label) now inserts the new entry's ID into
the cached set immediately after pushing the entry.

The cached set is initialized from existing entries during session open
(both JSONL and SQLite paths), rebuilt on save() and ensure_entry_ids(),
and populated during JSONL deserialization from the existing_ids set that
was already being computed.

Remove the now-unnecessary entry_id_exists() helper since all lookups go
through the HashSet.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
